_comment: Single source of truth for the field geometry + camera mounts. EDIT to match the real site. Coordinates in CENTIMETRES. Frame: origin (0,0) at corner pole A0; +x along the 40 ft LENGTH (0..1219.2); +y across the 20 ft WIDTH (0..609.6). calibration.py reads each landmark's coords FROM HERE so you only click in the image.
_wall_note: Paddock wall is 38.5 in = 97.79 cm tall. A '<pole>_top' landmark = that pole's (x,y) at z=97.79 (where the pole crosses the wall top). Used by 'pnp' cameras (CH03/CH04) that see too few/collinear ground poles - clicking each pole at the BASE (z=0) and the WALL-TOP crossing (z=97.79) gives non-coplanar points to solve full camera pose.
_poles_note: 15 poles on a 10 ft (304.8 cm) grid: 3 ROWS across the 20 ft width (A y=0, B y=304.8, C y=609.6) x 5 COLUMNS along the 40 ft length (0..4 at x=0,304.8,609.6,914.4,1219.2). B = centre row (length-wise centreline).
_markers_note: TEMPORARY ground markers (z=0) placed for CH03/CH04 calibration on the grass, from the hand sketch. 50 in = 127.0 cm, 15 in = 38.1 cm. M* = CH03 (x=0 end), referenced off pole B0=(0,304.8): M3 is 50in into the field (+x) from B0; M2/M4 are 50in toward A/C (-/+y); M6 is 15in past M3 (+x). N* = CH04 (x=1219.2 end), referenced off B4=(1219.2,304.8) with +x flipped (markers go toward field centre). M1/M5/N1/N5 (edge markers) are ESTIMATES at x=marker-row depth on the side walls - correct if measured. Remove this block after calibrating; markers are not permanent field structure.

shelters._comment: Each shelter footprint is 24-5/8 in x 18 in = 62.55 x 45.72 cm. Set center_cm (and orientation_deg if rotated) - easiest via place_cameras.py. Corners are derived. Anchors the nadir cams CH5/CH6.

camera_mounts._comment: Keys = RECORDING folders CH01..CH06 (= your CH1..CH6). pos_cm = ground position (the pole it sits on). height_cm ~ 8 ft. mapping: 'poly' (Duo3 panos) / 'pnp' (RLC-1212A side cams via wall) / 'homography' (RLC-520A shelter cams). Placeholders - set with place_cameras.py.
